item.title: Blocked drain, 18th June
item.description:
Drain is nearly at lowest part of chislehurst green Lane, so run-off is an issue. Drain is so blocked that it is almost invisible. Full of silt.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Lane, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Keep Right bollard damaged., 18th June
item.description:
The fluorescent "keep right" sign on the pedestrian crossing point outside of numbers 145 and 147 Manor Road, Erith has been struck by an unknown vehicle and ripped out of the ground. This is a popular point for local pedestrians to cross the very busy Manor Road, and the damage to the sign represents a danger to anyone using the crossing point.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Manor Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big deep hole in road, 18th June
item.description:
It's very deep. Looks like area of road collapsing

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Laurel Grove, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Dead duck in middle of island peter pains pond

item.title: Dead Fox, 18th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 76, Kynaston Road, Ramsden, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yes, 18th June
item.description:
Yes, these potholes have been there for the past three months. It’s causing a big problem with heavy lorries. It should not be allowed on these roads. If damage to the property is caused by these roads, somebody must be liable if they are not maintained also the speed limit should be 20 miles an hour. They are children from both side of the road and these lorries and cars are speeding.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 230, Erith Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox., 18th June
item.description:
Dead fox in gutter. Ive bagged and stored in bin
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lyveden Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous new road sign, 18th June
item.description:
A new speed indicator sign has been built in a dangerous and inappropriate location. Drivers will be distracted by the difficult interpreting the congestion charge warning sign. The congestion charge sign should be reattached to back of the new sign.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carnegie Street, Barnsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: To the side of Joe & the Juice in Hartland Road., 18th June
item.description:
They opened recently and locate their bins on the pavement (which I thought was not allowed) and they block the pavement. I cannot get a wheelchair between the bins and the lamppost. And since they put the bins there, there is constant fly-tipping. They have space to locate the bins in Hawley Mews at the back (as the previous owners did). The bins are collected by ‘First Mile’, not Violia. Please ask them to relocate the bins at the rear to free up the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chalk Farm Road, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Fly-tip Medium - 4 to 9 black bags problem has been found, 18th June
item.description:
A Fly-tip Medium - 4 to 9 black bags problem has been found by Brent Council

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cambridge Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Road needs urgent clearing from the road traffic fire, 18th June
item.description:
Resident reporting please could the road get cleared from all the debris, rubbish burnt out white goods as reported two weeks ago due to a fire
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Byron Drive, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 7 Barrett’s Grove ,, 18th June
item.description:
Property appears vacant after sale , rubbish attracting cats , dogs, foxes and birds . Has been like that for over a week.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Barretts Grove, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

A Fly-tip Medium - 4 to 9 black bags problem has been found by Brent Council

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Altash field, 18th June
item.description:
Both fields along Altash Way are being severely neglected and are in urgent need of maintenance. The first field does occasionally have its grass cut; however, the entrances are often left untouched, forcing residents to walk through heavily overgrown vegetation just to access the main grassed area. The benches are also becoming unusable, as they are surrounded and partially covered by long grass and weeds. The second field, adjacent to Altash Gardens, appears not to have been mowed for several months. The entire area has become an overgrown meadow and is effectively unusable for public recreation, except for the pathway leading to the tennis courts. Several benches within the field are now buried in long grass and weeds due to the lack of maintenance. This issue requires attention as soon as possible. With the warmer weather and increased use of outdoor spaces, local residents should be able to enjoy and make full use of this recreational area. I would appreciate it if this matter could be inspected and the necessary maintenance work arranged at the earliest opportunity.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Altash Way, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very uneven steps from bus stop down to Byron Close, 18th June
item.description:
Uneven steps from bus stop down to Byron Close. It has been like this for years but has started to detoriate and people have started stumbling. Report raised by the Moorings Neighborhood Forum.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Carlyle Road, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven road, 18th June
item.description:
The road is very uneven and when crossing it this morning my son fell over on the way to school by tripping over it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Frant Close, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamp post is not working and makes the footpath dark at night, 18th June
item.description:
A new lamppost was installed and it has never worked. The side access road down the side of my house is very dark and the light not working creates a security risk. The lamppost is the one nearest to my property 373 bedonwell road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bedonwell Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland Road, Hackney, London
